tools.name: cve_lookup
tools.description: Look up a CVE by ID and get a compact summary: description, CVSS score & severity, vector, CWE weakness, publish date, and references. Source: NVD (NIST).

tools.name: package_vulnerabilities
tools.description: List known vulnerabilities for a software package (optionally a specific version) via OSV. Ecosystems: npm, pypi, cargo, go, maven, rubygems, nuget, composer, pub, hex.

tools.name: audit_dependencies
tools.description: Audit a whole dependency manifest for known vulnerabilities in one call. Paste a package.json (as 'manifest'), or pass a 'dependencies' array of {name, version} objects. Returns per-package findings and a summary. Ecosystem defaults to npm.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "audit_dependencies" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Audit an entire dependency manifest in one call. Paste a package.json (npm), requirements.txt (PyPI), or go.mod (Go) and get a consolidated vulnerability report across every dependency via OSV.dev. Answers whether a project dependency tree is safe without checking packages one by one." → after: "Audit a whole dependency manifest for known vulnerabilities in one call. Paste a package.json (as 'manifest'), or pass a 'dependencies' array of {name, version} objects. Returns per-package findings and a summary. Ecosystem defaults to npm."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "cve_lookup" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Look up a CVE by ID. Returns a compact summary: description, CVSS score/severity, CISA KEV (known-exploited) status, affected products, dates, and top references. Use before approving dependencies, evaluating alerts, or assessing exposure." → after: "Look up a CVE by ID and get a compact summary: description, CVSS score & severity, vector, CWE weakness, publish date, and references. Source: NVD (NIST)."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "package_vulnerabilities" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "List known vulnerabilities for a software package (optionally a specific version) using OSV.dev. Covers npm, PyPI, Go, Maven, crates.io, RubyGems, Packagist, NuGet and more. Returns vuln IDs, severity, summary, and fixed versions." → after: "List known vulnerabilities for a software package (optionally a specific version) via OSV. Ecosystems: npm, pypi, cargo, go, maven, rubygems, nuget, composer, pub, hex."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool removed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "url_check" is no longer advertised. Removal can precede a shadowing replacement from another server.

findings.title: Tool removed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ip_reputation" is no longer advertised. Removal can precede a shadowing replacement from another server.
